  • India's Abhishek Sharma (AP Photo/Mahesh Kumar A.)

    'If Pakistan captain wants, we'll play him': SKY after Agha's comment on Abhishek
  • 'Victory is not declared by mere rhetoric': CDS Gen Anil Chauhan takes swipe at Pakistan

    Chief of Defence Staff General Anil Chauhan emphasized that "actual vijay lies in demonstrated evidence," referencing India's counterterror actions. He stressed that victory isn't built on rhetoric but on tangible outcomes like damaged terror infrastructure and dysfunctional air defence systems, urging a sober assessment of India's defense preparedness for future challenges.

  • How Parkinson’s could be read decades before tremors show up

    Disturbed sleep, constipation and mood changes may be early disease markers but not diagnostic certainties

  • Horrific Valentine’s Day murder in Noida: Man shoots woman inside car, then turns gun on himself

    A man and a woman were discovered dead from gunshot wounds inside a locked car in Noida's Sector 107. Police found a pistol in the man's hand, suggesting a possible murder-suicide. The deceased have been identified, and their families have been notified. Investigations are ongoing to determine the exact sequence of events.

  • 'Interest of our people come first': What BNP chief said on Bangladesh's foreign policy on India
  • Not India vs Pakistan anymore, it's all about Usman Tariq’s bowling action in Colombo

    Ahead of the India-Pakistan blockbuster, the spotlight has shifted from rivalry to scrutiny, with Usman Tariq’s bowling action dominating discourse and unsettling both batters and press rooms in Colombo.

  • Rahul called India-US deal a 'surrender'. But it's not — here’s why

    As Washington recalibrates its language on the interim India-US trade deal, the $500 billion trade target has sparked sharp political debate at home. While Rahul Gandhi calls it a “surrender,” exporters and trade officials argue the numbers are achievable — and that steady diplomacy, not theatrics, will define the outcome

  • US FTC to probe Microsoft's business practices

    US regulators are intensifying their probe into Microsoft's alleged monopolistic practices in cloud computing and AI. The FTC is investigating if the tech giant hinders rivals by scrutinizing licensing and bundling of services like Copilot. This mirrors past concerns raised by Google, though their EU complaint was withdrawn.

  • 'You captured my heart': WH releases Valentine’s Day cards featuring Maduro & more
  • Last-minute, meaningful Valentine’s gifts in 30 minutes with Myntra M-Now
  • Relive the 90s Live: ‘Cassettes–CDs Nostalgia’ concert is coming to your city; check dates, artists and other details
  • SIR murder in Bengal: BLO calls lover's husband to discuss roll revision; kills him
  • Ajit Pawar death: Rohit Pawar writes to Amit Shah for plane crash probe
  • After CEO asked employees to stop coding, Nvidia engineers get access to OpenAI's Codex

    Nvidia has deployed OpenAI's Codex AI coding tool to all 30,000 engineers, marking a significant enterprise rollout. CEO Jensen Huang's mandate to automate tasks with AI is driving this adoption, with engineers praising the tool's ability to handle complex workflows and maintain context.

  • Virgo, Weekly Horoscope, February 15 to February 21, 2026: Experience a productive week ahead

    Virgos will experience a productive week with sharp minds and improved timing, especially early on. Midweek brings caution regarding hidden issues and office politics, requiring private plans and firm boundaries. The week concludes with strong relationship support, legal aid, and alliances, emphasizing peace over arguments.

Opinion and Features

The Voice of Conscience

The charkha as a weapon
Margaret Bourke-White
(Photographer)
TOI take:
Published on: Jan 22, 2026
While imprisoned at Yeravda Jail in Pune from 1932 to 1933, Mahatma Gandhi turned confinement into resistance, urging Indians to spin their own cloth as a rejection of British rule. When photographer Margaret Bourke-White arrived to capture his portrait, she was first required to learn how to use a charkha. The iconic portrait of Gandhi reading beside his wheel became an enduring symbol of his pacifist philosophy and non-violent civil disobedience.
'The last Jew in Vinnitsa'
1941
TOI take:
Published on: Jan 21, 2026
Found in a German soldier’s album, this 1941 photograph titled "The Last Jew in Vinnitsa" captures an Einsatzgruppe D member executing a Ukrainian man. This chilling image documents the Holocaust in Ukraine, where nearly one million Jews were murdered by Nazi forces, including the German Army and auxiliary groups.
A satellite’s unlikely ride
1981
TOI take:
Published on: Nov 21, 2025
On June 19, 1981, Isro launched its first communication satellite, Apple, aboard Ariane-1 from Kourou in French Guiana. This satellite, iconically, was transported on a bullock cart—a reflection of Isro's humble beginnings—just six years after Aryabhatta, when both the agency and the nation’s infrastructure were still developing.
